prompt: A CivicCrowd Reservation & Crowdfunding Management System that includes patron-facing booking and contribution flows plus internal governance for reviews, risk controls, and operational messaging. Regular Users browse locally published crowdfunding campaigns and venue programs, select reward tiers or reserve time-slot-based seating for on-site events, and receive immediate in-app confirmations, voucher displays, and status updates in a single Notification Center with language and time-zone aware templates (for example, an English and Spanish receipt rendered in the user’s preferred time zone). The booking UI, built with Laravel Livewire components for real-time feedback without page reloads, shows availability by time slot and seat count, clearly indicates when seats are temporarily held, and blocks duplicate submissions with a visible “processing” state and a final confirmation number. Users can manage the full order lifecycle from one screen: create an order, mark it as paid via offline tender logging (cash/card-on-file recorded by staff, no online payment), cancel within policy, request refunds or after-sales adjustments, track logistics milestones for locally fulfilled rewards, and view electronic vouchers that can be scanned on-site. Project Creators draft campaigns with target amounts (e.g., $25,000.00) and fixed durations (7–60 days), add risk disclosures and reward tiers, then submit for review; Moderators approve and control online/offline visibility and enforce status transitions from draft to pending review to fundraising to success/failure to closed. Both Creators and Users can leave two-way reviews after fulfillment using star dimensions plus tags and text, with anti-retaliation rules such as delayed display for 72 hours and masked identities on public views.The backend uses Laravel to enforce workflows, validations, and audit trails, with PostgreSQL persisting users, campaigns, reward tiers, orders, vouchers, seat inventories, reviews, and risk events entirely on local storage for offline operation. Availability and seat-locking are calculated per time slot with conflict detection; seat locks have a 5-minute TTL, automatically release on expiration, and booking submissions are idempotent by client-generated request key to prevent double charges/holds even after refresh. Order state machines restrict illegal transitions (for example, cancellation is allowed until 2 hours before the scheduled start; refund requests must be filed within 14 days of fulfillment; after-sales requests require staff notes and an attachment that is validated by file type and checksum, not cloud scanning). Security includes local username/password login only, salted password hashing (Argon2id), JWT or server sessions with rotation and inactivity timeout (30 minutes for staff, 2 hours for users), and CSRF protection for browser clients; sensitive fields such as phone numbers and mailing addresses are encrypted at rest and masked in UI unless the role is authorized. The Notification Center is implemented as an in-app inbox and on-screen alerts generated from template rules and event subscriptions (application decisions, order status changes, receipts, reminders), without SMS/email/push dependencies; administrators can configure dictionaries and business parameters (cancellation windows, lock TTL, reminder lead times) and enable/disable integration stubs, while webhook definitions remain local-only for future on-prem connectivity but are not required for core functionality. Risk control calculates a credit score from no-shows, chargebacks/refunds, and violations, supports gray/black restrictions (e.g., blacklisted users cannot place new orders for 90 days), flags anomalies like frequent withdrawals (more than 3 refund requests in 30 days) or multiple accounts on the same device fingerprint, and routes evidence-based disputes to an arbitration queue with immutable decision logs.
